task: #27 P9.C1 compact MoE + Q8_0 WMMA prefill tile sweep
summary: P9.C1 selected-MoE/Q8_0 WMMA tuning retained partial improvements but missed the <=110 ms combined WMMA bucket gate. Best retained default now: Q4_K selected dual 32x16, Q5_K/Q6_K selected down legacy 16x16, selected launch-bound min-blocks 2, Q8_0 shape-aware single tiles (2048x8192 and 2048x4096 -> 16x32; 4096x2048 -> 64x32; 2048x512 -> 16x32), Q8_0 shared gate+up dual concat 16x32. Combined Q4 dual + Q5/Q6 down + all Q8_0 WMMA bucket is 139.44 ms (target <=110 ms), down from 170.41 ms (~18.2%).

hardware.gpu: AMD Radeon RX 7900 XTX / gfx1100 (local; W7900 not re-run)

software.git_status_at_measurement:
## gguf-bulk-prefill
 M hipengine/kernels/hip_gfx1100/quant/gguf_q8_0_prefill.py
 M scripts/qwen35_gguf_rocprof_summary.py
 M tests/test_gguf_q8_0_wmma_prefill.py
 M tests/test_qwen35_gguf_rocprof_summary.py
?? uv.lock

commands.tests: HIPENGINE_COMPILER_VERSION_FILE=/tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt uv run --with pytest pytest tests/test_gguf_q8_0_wmma_prefill_dual.py tests/test_gguf_q8_0_wmma_prefill.py tests/test_gguf_q4_k_selected_wmma_prefill.py tests/test_gguf_k_selected_wmma_prefill.py tests/test_gguf_gemv_decode_dispatch.py tests/test_qwen35_gguf_compact_moe_gemv_routing.py -q --no-header

correctness.evidence: 136-test adjacent bundle passed (Q8_0 dual/single, Q4_K/Q5_K/Q6_K selected WMMA fixtures, dispatch routing, compact GEMV routing). P8.4/P8.5 selected WMMA CPU-reference fixtures still pass.

notes: single-csv mode: only the prefill phase is reported. Pass --decode-csv (paired with --prefill-csv) to also report a decode phase.

q4_k_selected_dual.reason: best retained qwen35moe rocprof result; 58.56 ms vs ~65.66 ms legacy under Q8 dual

q5_k_selected_down.reason: multi-tile variants were slower in E2E sweeps

q6_k_selected_down.reason: multi-tile variants were slower in E2E sweeps

q8_0_shared_gate_up_dual.reason: dual concat path; reduces Q8_0 dispatches 250 -> 170

q8_0_single_out_ge_4096.reason: P9.C1 microbench: shared-expert single out=4096 ~2x faster than 32x32

selected_launch_bounds_min_blocks.reason: P9.C1 launch-bounds sweep retained the P8 __launch_bounds__(32,2) hint.

q8_0_single_shape_2048x8192.reason: P9.C1 targeted shape sweep: linear-attn qkv / full-attn q+gate shape best at 16x32 (0.538 ms synthetic vs 0.818 ms for 64x32).

q8_0_single_shape_2048x4096.reason: P9.C1 targeted shape sweep: linear-attn gate shape slightly best at 16x32.

q8_0_single_shape_4096x2048.reason: P9.C1 targeted shape sweep: ssm/shared-down shape best at 64x32.

q8_0_single_shape_2048x512.reason: P9.C1 targeted shape sweep: full-attn k/v shape best at 16x32.

launch_bounds_sweep.reason: min-blocks=2 is the fastest single-run setting and matches the legacy P8 launch-bound hint; min-blocks=1 gives no benefit, while 4/8 are slower.

routing_distribution_512_0_repeated_toke.interpretation: Routing is highly skewed: only ~19% of experts are nonzero; hot experts often have hundreds of rows while most nonzero experts have tiny tails. Padding is measurable (13-18%) but insufficient alone to close the ~29 ms acceptance gap.

tail_no_padding_hybrid.evidence: wmma padded rows exceed compact rows by 13.0% for Q5 layers and 18.5% for Q6 layers; ideal removal saves at most ~11 ms of the 87 ms selected-MoE bucket, below the ~29 ms residual gap.
tail_no_padding_hybrid.next_if_pursued: Use WMMA for full 16-row expert tiles and compact GEMV/tail kernel for <16-row tails; useful as secondary optimization only.

bulk_compact_gemv_no_padding.evidence: Temporary rows>1 route through existing P9.B compact GEMV kernels: 512/0 prefill 1.958 s = 261.49 tok/s, far slower than retained WMMA path (~0.305 s / 1678 tok/s).

expert_pack8_sidecar_with_wmma_prefill.evidence: Benchmark with --use-expert-sidecar --preload-expert-sidecars and WMMA prefill still used raw selected WMMA kernels; 3-run median 1648.13 tok/s, below retained 1678.00 tok/s.

awq_pack8_wmma_sidecar_proxy.evidence: Synthetic same-routing proxy using existing PARO/AWQ pack8 WMMA: Q4 dual layer 6.65 ms and down layer 3.44 ms, much slower than raw selected WMMA average (Q4 dual ~1.44 ms/layer; Q5 down ~0.73 ms/layer).
awq_pack8_wmma_sidecar_proxy.caveat: This tests the existing AWQ layout/kernel as a proxy, not a custom GGUF-K sidecar WMMA design.

selected_tm_tn_and_launch_bounds.evidence: Previous P9.C1 sweeps retained Q4 32x16, Q5/Q6 legacy 16x16, launch_bounds min-blocks 2. Generic multi-tile variants increase register pressure and slow down except Q4 32x16.

most_fruitful_next_paths_ranked.path: custom raw GGUF-K selected WMMA redesign for hot experts
most_fruitful_next_paths_ranked.why: Only path with enough headroom: selected-MoE needs ~1.34x improvement (87.3 -> ~58 ms). Focus on Q4 dual (57.7 ms) and Q5 down (26.8 ms), not scheduler/copy/silu.
most_fruitful_next_paths_ranked.design_sketch: Specialize for skewed routing: hot experts with >=64 rows use a lower-register-pressure raw kernel with predecoded per-block scale/min sidecar or split Q4 scale/min decode from WMMA inner loop; sparse tails use existing legacy/tail path. Preserve compact ABI and CPU-reference fixtures.
most_fruitful_next_paths_ranked.test_plan: Prototype one Q4 hot-expert kernel for rows multiple-of-16/full experts using the real expert_start map; target Q4 dual <40 ms before touching Q5.

most_fruitful_next_paths_ranked.why: Can recover padding/tiny-expert overhead but max ideal saving is ~11 ms, so it cannot hit target alone.
most_fruitful_next_paths_ranked.test_plan: Add tail GEMV kernel only for experts with count % 16 != 0 or count < 16; measure selected-MoE bucket delta.

most_fruitful_next_paths_ranked.path: new GGUF-K sidecar/repack WMMA (not existing AWQ/pack8 GEMV)
most_fruitful_next_paths_ranked.why: Existing AWQ proxy and pack8 GEMV sidecar are slower; a useful sidecar would need to preserve GGUF-K math while reducing Q4 scale/min decode and register pressure.
most_fruitful_next_paths_ranked.test_plan: Microbench one layer with preexpanded Q4 scale/min sidecar before integrating runtime materialization.

acceptance.reason: Combined WMMA bucket 139.44 ms > 110 ms target. Shape-aware Q8_0 tiles reduced the gap materially, but remaining ~29 ms appears dominated by selected Q4/Q5 down kernels and Q8_0 large projection cost; previous TM/TN and launch-bounds sweeps show the generic raw selected multi-tile path cannot close it.
